text: What It Is
llmwiki is a knowledge compiler: it ingests raw URLs or local files and turns them into an interlinked Markdown wiki.
In this repo, that compiler is packaged with a local WebUI, an Electron desktop shell, and companion review/sync tooling around the compiled wiki workflow.
Who It Is For
Primary persona: AI researchers, engineers, and technical writers who want a persistent, browsable knowledge base instead of repeatedly rediscovering scattered sources.
What It Does
Ingests a URL or local file into sources/.
Detects changed sources and skips unchanged inputs.
Extracts concepts with an LLM and generates Markdown concept pages.
Adds source attribution and rebuilds wiki/index.md plus wiki/MOC.md.
Resolves inter-page [[wikilinks]].
Answers questions against the wiki; --save stores answers as reusable pages.
Exposes watch, lint, and local Web/Desktop browsing workflows.
How It Works
CLI (src/cli.ts): ingest, compile, query, watch, lint.
Compiler (src/compiler/index.ts): lock -> hash/diff -> extract concepts -> consolidate claims, episodes, and procedures -> write pages -> rebuild navigation.
Local web server (web/server/index.ts): Express app serving client assets plus APIs for search, review, chat, graph, automation, flash diary, source gallery, and remote-brain actions.
Desktop shell (desktop-webui/src/main.ts): starts the local server, loads it in Electron, and manages startup config, shortcuts, and initialization/sync flows.
Data flow: raw sources -> sources/ or sources_full -> compile -> wiki/ plus .llmwiki state -> query, search, review, and UI views.
How to Run
Install Node.js >= 18.
Run npm install.
Set provider credentials. Default Anthropic: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN. OpenAI path: set LLMWIKI_PROVIDER=openai and OPENAI_API_KEY.
Run npm run build.
CLI quick start: node dist/cli.js ingest SOURCE, then node dist/cli.js compile, then node dist/cli.js query "your question".
Optional local UI path: npm run desktop:webui:launch. Single canonical desktop onboarding guide: Not found in repo.
